title: Flank pain

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

assessment.text: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

source.title: Renal and ureteric stones: assessment and management
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with suspected or confirmed renal or ureteric stones, with age and pregnancy qualifiers.
source.setting: Assessment, imaging, procedural management, metabolic testing, prevention, and follow-up of renal and ureteric stones.
source.exclusions: Exact only for selected stone identity, imaging, stone-analysis, metabolic-workup, hydration-context, and clinician-discussed prevention documentation. Acute symptom assessment, treatment, procedures, fixed follow-up, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-reviewed CT or ultrasound imaging with age and pregnancy qualifiers.
